help.title: Team API Key
help.markdown:
To create or retrieve new Team API Key, navigate to [Account & Profile](https://ui.honeycomb.io/account)

Ensure key has the following permissions:

- Manage Queries and Columns
- Run Queries

fields.label: URL
fields.description: Customize the api URL. By default this will be https://api.honeycomb.io

validations.message: URL must use the https scheme

fields.label: Team Name
fields.description: Specify the team name. This will be useful in data links

fields.label: Environment Name
fields.description: Optional. Specify the environment name. This will be useful in data links

fields.label: Time Window (days)
fields.description: Optional. The maximum time window, in days. Queries will only return data from the last N days, where N is the retention limit. Default is 7 days, since that is the maximum retention limit normally supported by the Honeycomb API. Honeycomb API docs: https://api-docs.honeycomb.io/api/query-data

relationships.description: API connection: the Honeycomb API base URL (jsonData.hostname) and the API key (secureJsonData.apiKey), which is sent as the X-Honeycomb-Team header on every request (pkg/main.go:46-51; pkg/httpclient/client.go:39-42).

Honeycomb UI URL for the 'Open in Honeycomb' data link: the API hostname with its literal 'api' substring replaced by 'ui', followed by /<team>[/environments/<environment>]/datasets/<dataset>/result/<id> (pkg/plugin/querydata.go:200-216; src/Datasource.ts:109-136).

instructions.msg: The Honeycomb datasource has a single authentication method: an API key stored in secureJsonData.apiKey. It is sent as the 'X-Honeycomb-Team' header on every outgoing request (pkg/httpclient/client.go:39-42). There are no other auth methods. The key must be a Honeycomb Team API key with the 'Run Queries' and 'Manage Queries and Columns' permissions; the health check fails otherwise (pkg/plugin/healthcheck.go:28-38 checks api_key_access['queries'] and api_key_access['columns']).

Minimal working payload: {"jsonData":{"hostname":"https://api.honeycomb.io","team":"<your-honeycomb-team-slug>"},"secureJsonData":{"apiKey":"<your-honeycomb-api-key>"}}. jsonData.hostname, jsonData.team, and secureJsonData.apiKey are all required; jsonData.environment and jsonData.retentionLimit are optional. The backend Validate rejects a missing/empty hostname ('enter a URL'), a non-https hostname ('invalid url: scheme must be https'), a missing API key ('enter an API key'), and a missing team ('enter a Honeycomb team name') (pkg/models/settings.go:45-71).

secureJsonData.apiKey is write-only: it can be set or reset but never read back. To check whether the key is already configured on an existing datasource, read secureJsonFields.apiKey (boolean) instead of secureJsonData.apiKey.

jsonData.hostname is the Honeycomb API base URL and must use the https scheme (pkg/models/settings.go:52-60). It defaults to https://api.honeycomb.io (US) when absent (pkg/models/settings.go:28); use the region-specific host for other Honeycomb instances (for example https://api.eu1.honeycomb.io for EU). The backend uses it as the request base URL and REPLACES its path per request (pkg/requestor/requestor.go:76-81), so a trailing path is discarded rather than concatenated.

jsonData.team (required) and jsonData.environment (optional) are used only to build the 'Open in Honeycomb' data-link URLs; they are NOT part of API authentication (that is the apiKey alone). The link is <hostname with 'api'->'ui'>/<team>[/environments/<environment>]/datasets/<dataset>/result/<id> (pkg/plugin/querydata.go:200-216; src/Datasource.ts:109-136). team is the Honeycomb team slug used in the URL path, not the display name. Even though it only affects data links, the health check fails without it.

jsonData.retentionLimit is a number of days (default 7) that clamps query start times to the last N days; when a panel's time range extends past it, results are clipped and a 'Partial results' warning is attached (pkg/plugin/querydata.go:281-318). Only raise it above 7 if your Honeycomb plan retains more than 7 days of data.
